sections.paragraph: an unused root, which had, I expect, the same meaning as the kindred word שֵׁרֵשׁ (denom. from שֹׁרֶשׁ), to pull up by the roots; hence to extirpate, specially the testicles, to castrate. Syr. and Chald. ܣܰܪܶܣ סָרֵס to castrate. Hence סָרִיס سريس castrated (prop. extirpated); whence the secondary verb سَِرسَ to be sexually impotent.
